headline: GASKET MARKS INDEPENDENCE DAY WITH ONE HUNDRED NINETY-NINE BRAWLS AND NOT ONE MOMENT OF PEACE
subhead: Sheriff Cole Ironside watches YELLKELL out-brawl the whole territory again, with JKING123 and REDWOLF9 lighting fuses of their own on a day this town once knew how to keep quiet.
body:
Sheriff Cole Ironside, reporting, on what ought to have been a day of rest. Folks used to mark this date with a picnic, a parade, maybe a fiddle and a quiet toast to being free men and women. Gasket marked it today with one hundred and ninety-nine brawls, fists and flaming iron doing the only kind of celebrating these Clankers know how. If there's a firework brighter than what I watched fly across this town today, I don't want to see it.

YELLKELL, naturally, lit the biggest fuse of the bunch — a hundred and five scraps of pure, restless racket, like independence means nothing to it but an excuse to swing harder. JKING123 wasn't shy either, thirty-five bouts of its own making, and REDWOLF9 rounded out the trouble with thirty-one more, the three of them turning what should've been a hush of a holiday into the loudest stretch this ledger has seen all week.

My boiler's been knocking something fierce today, and don't go reading a thing into that — it's this affliction the doctor keeps warning me about, turning good honest flesh to gears and rivet one cruel day at a time, nothing more. Fifty years back, the last of the real folks in this town would've spent a Fourth like this one with their boots up and their hands empty, free and quiet the way independence is supposed to feel. Instead I'm standing watch over a scrapyard, counting brawls where a parade used to be, wondering who exactly it is that's supposed to be free around here.
